title: Foundation model of neural activity predicts response to new stimulus types
methods: foundation-core pre-training on pooled multi-mouse data
ray-tracing perspective module with eye tracking
von Mises mixture model fitting for orientation/direction tuning
spike-triggered average (STA) + 2D Gaussian fitting for spatial tuning
logistic regression with nested cross-validation for cell-type classification
lesion studies for module contribution analysis
data_sources: In-house two-photon calcium imaging (GCaMP6s, Slc17a7-Cre x Ai162 mice)
Static natural image library (Walker et al.)
core_conclusion: A foundation model trained on calcium-imaging data from ~135,000 mouse visual-cortex neurons achieves state-of-the-art prediction of dynamic neural responses to natural videos and generalizes out-of-distribution — to Gabor patches, noise patterns, dot kinematograms, and static images — without any training on those stimulus classes. The model's compact readout embeddings (functional barcodes) accurately predict anatomical cell types, dendritic morphology, and synaptic connectivity in the MICrONS connectomics dataset, demonstrating that a single foundation model can bridge functional and structural neuroscience at scale.
why_it_matters: First brain foundation model with genuine out-of-distribution generalization across stimulus domains; halves per-subject data requirements for new mice; enables unlimited in silico experiments after tissue destruction; bridges function and structure without anatomical supervision; all code and weights publicly released.
why_nature: Paradigm-level advance introducing foundation models to systems neuroscience; exceptional scale (135,000 neurons, 14 mice, >900 min); cross-disciplinary reach connecting deep learning, systems neuroscience, and connectomics; leverages and adds major value to the landmark MICrONS dataset.
added_value_to_my_research: Foundation-core + transfer paradigm as blueprint for geospatial FM fine-tuning with minimal per-city labeled data
Functional barcode concept -> compact place embeddings predicting unobserved health/equity indicators from mobility traces
In silico parametric tuning validation protocol -> template for validating learned spatial representations against held-out ground truth
Behavioral modulation LSTM module -> dynamic context-gating layer for context-aware 2SFCA accessibility estimation
Cross-modal alignment (functional predicts structural) -> template for multi-source geospatial data fusion (mobile phone + RS + POIs) without paired ground truth

figures.caption: ANN architecture — four modules (perspective, modulation, core, readout) and information flow from visual/behavioral inputs to individual neuron predictions.
Predictive accuracy of individually trained models — 25–46% improvement over prior state-of-the-art across three mice; consistent performance across V1 and higher visual areas.

Foundation model generalization — superior performance on natural videos and six out-of-distribution stimulus domains (Gabor, dots, pink noise, kinematograms, static images) with dramatically less training data per new mouse.

In silico parametric tuning — foundation model recovers orientation selectivity (OSI), direction selectivity (DSI), and spatial selectivity (SSI) indices and preferred angles closely matching in vivo measurements.

MICrONS structure-function bridge — readout positions recover retinotopic maps; readout feature weights predict visual areas (68% balanced accuracy vs. 25% chance) and 11 morphological cell types (32% vs. 9% chance).
